task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Create at least three vertex points on the canvas
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three distinct points (e.g., A, B, C) are visible on the GeoGebra geometry canvas or listed in the Algebra view.

task_completion.description: Use the Polygon tool to create the triangle
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Polygon tool is selected from the toolbar, and a shaded polygon connecting the three vertices is visible on the canvas. The Algebra view should show a Polygon object (not just individual line segments).

task_completion.description: Ensure the triangle is equilateral (equal sides, 60-degree angles)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The constructed triangle visually appears equilateral. The Algebra view may show equal lengths for the three sides or 60-degree angles if the angle measurement tool was used.

task_completion.description: Save the file as 'equilateral_triangle.ggb' in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog box is visible with the filename 'equilateral_triangle.ggb' and the path '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/', or a terminal/file manager confirms the file exists in this location.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the triangle
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking tools in the GeoGebra toolbar and interacting with the canvas, rather than writing a script or terminal commands to generate the .ggb file.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual GeoGebra application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches GeoGebra (with its specific Algebra and Graphics views), not a generic drawing tool or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent constructed the triangle from scratch
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the progressive creation of points and the polygon, rather than just opening a pre-existing 'equilateral_triangle.ggb' file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires an equilateral triangle with exactly 3 vertices and 60° angles (±2° tolerance). The agent MUST use the Polygon tool to connect the vertices; the strict verifier will reject the solution if only line segments are used without a polygon object. The final file must be saved exactly as 'equilateral_triangle.ggb' in ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/.
